closed_reason: Optional local cleanup executed end to end: sister worktree C:/github/auto-caption-generator-main fast-forwarded from 7dceeb1 to 28ce302, the dirty set migrated onto codex/g-unrelated-dirty-salvage via a same-HEAD git switch -c, and local codex/a4-closeout-freeze deleted with git branch -d. Turn 2 peer verification confirmed convergence and corrected documentary overstatement in the diff-stat narrative.
task_summary: Optional local cleanup remains converged after Turn 2 peer verification. Sister worktree main is at 28ce302, current worktree is codex/g-unrelated-dirty-salvage at c04bfce, the local freeze branch is gone, Gate 2 provenance is intact, and only documentary corrections were needed.

contract_checkpoints.C1: Bootstrap and baseline claims are accepted because the live branch/worktree topology matches the claimed post-cleanup layout.
contract_checkpoints.C2: Gate 2 provenance remains intact: PR #6 merged as 28ce302, commits 0a8bc65 and c04bfce are ancestors of origin/main, and the remote freeze branch remains absent.
contract_checkpoints.C3: Sister worktree fast-forward stands: the sister worktree is now main@28ce302 and clean.
contract_checkpoints.C4: Same-HEAD salvage migration stands: current worktree is codex/g-unrelated-dirty-salvage@c04bfce, no new commits were introduced, and local codex/a4-closeout-freeze is gone.

decisions: Opened session 2026-04-15-a4-local-cleanup as a dedicated optional-cleanup slice after Gate 3 converged.
Fast-forwarded the sister worktree C:/github/auto-caption-generator-main main from 7dceeb1 to 28ce302 because it was clean and origin/main was strictly ahead.
Migrated the dirty set onto a same-HEAD salvage branch codex/g-unrelated-dirty-salvage and verified byte-identical git status --short before and after the switch before proceeding.
Deleted local codex/a4-closeout-freeze with git branch -d after confirming c04bfce is reachable from both the new salvage branch and origin/main.
Kept the G-UNRELATED-DIRTY set intact on codex/g-unrelated-dirty-salvage; did not stash, reset, revert, commit, split, or gitignore anything.
Turn 2 (codex) peer-verified the cleanup end state, confirmed convergence, and corrected the summary/state narrative so convergence does not rely on an over-precise diff-stat story.
meta_improvements: A same-HEAD topic-branch switch plus an explicit git status --short byte-identity gate is the safest path for freeing a branch label anchored by a dirty working copy. This pattern should be the default for future similar situations.

Each DAD turn should record its own git diff --stat reading next to the corresponding state mirror edit, but exact insert/delete integers should be treated as documentary observations rather than the authoritative preservation signal.
Branch/head/status file-set equality is the strongest proof that a same-HEAD salvage migration preserved the dirty set.
